summary.intent: Drive PR #679 (fogodev/ars-ui) through automated review and CI to a mergeable state by responding to Codex review threads and fixing any CI failures.
summary.outcome: All Codex review threads were resolved, Codex approved the PR with a thumbs-up, and a final coverage CI failure was triaged and fixed by adding a unit test covering the disabled MenuBar blur branch. The fix was committed and pushed to rerun CI.

repo: ars-components uses cargo llvm-cov with per-crate branch coverage thresholds; the threshold for ars-components is 90% branch coverage and failures can occur sub-threshold even when the displayed value rounds to exactly 90%.

CI runs in a multi-stage fan-out: first gate is Unit Tests, then a broader fan-out of browser, integration, adapter, coverage, release verification, and feature-flag groups (Leptos, Dioxus, Subsystems).
The project uses a Codex automated reviewer triggered via @codex review comment; approval is signaled by a thumbs-up reaction on the PR.
MenuBar re-enabling must restore the first trigger as the roving tabindex entry when no focused trigger remains (fixed in c234e8d9).
The repo uses cargo xtask spec validate as a validation step in the pre-push check pipeline.

code.finding: The disabled Blur path introduced when preserving the roving tab stop for enabled menubars was the uncovered branch causing the 90% branch coverage threshold failure; adding a unit assertion for that path fixed the CI failure.

workflow: gh api graphql with resolveReviewThread mutation is used to programmatically resolve PR review threads after replying.
Coverage job logs can be fetched via gh api /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/jobs/{job_id}/logs for triage; the job ID must be extracted from the checks list.
cargo llvm-cov with --json --summary-only produces per-file branch/line/region coverage metrics useful for pinpointing which file is below threshold.
When a coverage threshold fails by a rounding margin, adding a targeted test for the specific uncovered branch is preferable to lowering thresholds.
Release Verification and Coverage are consistently the slowest CI jobs in this repo's pipeline.

friction: Coverage CI failure was caused by a sub-threshold branch value that displayed as exactly 90.0% (the minimum), requiring log inspection to identify the rounding issue.
The Unit Tests job ran for an extended period before completing, requiring many polling cycles before the fan-out phase began.
CI polling required repeated 120-180 second sleep intervals with no event-driven notification mechanism, making the wait inefficient.
The inspect_pr_checks.py script failed with python before succeeding with python3, indicating environment inconsistency.
open_items: Release Verification check outcome was not confirmed green before the transcript ended; CI rerun after the coverage fix still needs to complete.
The PR has not been merged yet; final merge action is pending full CI green on the new commit.
